title: carpoxis retusa
intro: Carpoxis retusa, also known as the Desert Ironwood, is a small, deciduous tree native to the Sonoran Desert in North America. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which has been found to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Carpois retusa include its ability to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and even cancer, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. The herbal preparations of Carpois retusa include teas, infusions, and tinctures made from its bark, which can be used to support immune function and overall well-being.
